claims.statement: Sine, cosine, and tangent are related trigonometric functions, with tangent equal to sine divided by cosine where cosine is nonzero.
claims.scope: Real-angle trigonometry used for right triangles and unit-circle coordinates.
claims.uncertainty: Complex arguments and analytic continuation are outside scope.

claims.statement: A plane angle of one degree equals pi/180 radians, so a half turn is 180 degrees or pi radians.
claims.uncertainty: Rounding numerical angle values can slightly perturb reconstructed lengths.

claims.statement: For a real angle measured from the positive horizontal axis, cosine and sine give the horizontal and vertical coordinates on the unit circle, implying sine squared plus cosine squared equals one.
claims.scope: Standard unit-circle interpretation over real angles.
claims.uncertainty: Orientation conventions must be stated when a field measures angles from a different reference direction.

claims.statement: Inverse trigonometric functions return principal angles; geometric context and quadrant information are needed to interpret an intended direction beyond that principal range.
claims.scope: Real inverse sine, cosine, and tangent calculations in the lesson.
claims.uncertainty: Alternative software notation can use a superscript minus one ambiguously for inverse versus reciprocal functions.
